common_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_column' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
Add feed_start_date and feed_end_date to feed_info.txt and include calendar end dates in your export.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_required_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'missing_calendar_and_calendar_date_files' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
Check the vehicle positions endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
Review the rule documentation for 'trip_distance_exceeds_shape_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
Re-generate shape distances in your export; flag to your vendor if it persists.
